question: According to Table 1.2-1, what is the thermal power rating of each NuScale Power Module?

nuscale_ch01_0810.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.4 Detect and Suppress Method: Exclusion zone and buffer region methodology
nuscale_ch01_0810.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or buffer region methodology for thermal hydraulic stability, as reactor trip signals are designed to prevent conditions that could lead to unstable flow oscillations. Instead, the existing reactor trip system (RTS) provides protection by tripping the reactor before safety limits are violated, making additional detect and suppress (D&S) methods or backup options unnecessary. The RTS system partially conforms to regulatory criteria by preventing instability conditions, but it does not actively detect or suppress instabilities.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing its main systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly, while security-related details are withheld. The section also references figures illustrating the plant layout and system boundaries.

nuscale_ch01_0811.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.5 Detect and Suppress Method Trip of reactor before SAFDL violation
nuscale_ch01_0811.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or buffer region methodology for thermal hydraulic stability, as existing reactor trip signals already prevent violation of safety limits before unstable flow oscillations can develop. The system partially conforms to regulatory criteria by ensuring reactor trips occur before SAFDL violations, but does not employ detect and suppress methods or related instrumentation, as instabilities are not directly detected or suppressed. Backup options are not required due to the reliability of existing trip signals and technical specifications.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both discuss the NuScale design's approach to thermal hydraulic stability by describing the exclusion of the exclusion zone methodology and the use of reactor trip signals to prevent SAFDL and CHF violations, with Section B specifying the trip before SAFDL violation as a protective measure referenced in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the plant design, which includes operating characteristics such as thermal power rating, while Section B discusses regulatory criteria for thermal hydraulic stability that depend on the plant's operating parameters, including thermal power.

Section A provides a general description of the plant design, including operating characteristics such as thermal power, while Section B references Section 1.2 (from Section A) as the source identifying certified design features, thus linking the general plant description to the interface requirements.

Section B references Section 1.2, which contains Table 1.2-1—the source for the thermal power rating asked about in the analysis question—while Section A discusses safety analysis methods but does not provide or reference the specific design value.
